title: steiropteris decussata var. mapiriensis
intro: Steiropteris decussata var. mapiriensis, also known as Mapiri fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Blechnaceae, characterized by its distinctive decussate leaf arrangement. The Mapiri fern has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Steiropteris decussata var. mapiriensis include its potential to treat wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the Mapiri fern is often used as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be dried and used as a powder to be applied topically or ingested as a supplement.
